channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: 2026 Commencement Program Preferred Name
item.description: If you would like your preferred name (instead of your legal name) to appear in the 2026 Commencement program, please send an email to graduation@rpi.edu with your preference with the subject line “2026 Commencement Program Preferred Name”.To ensure your request can be accommodated, you must notify the Registrar’s Office no later than Thursday, April 23, 2026.If the Registrar does not receive a response by this deadline, they will use your legal name in the program.
item.author: Students

item.title: “Design, Innovation, and Society” alumni ASK US ANYTHING session!
item.description: Join a session with Design, Innovation, and Society alumni to discuss post-graduation life. Hear from recent and seasoned graduates about their careers and take this unique opportunity to ask them the questions you always wanted to ask about handling professional lives in the design worlds. 4/22 at 1pm in Sage 2211.
item.author: Students

item.title: IBM “Road to Practitioner” Quantum Program
item.description: IBM has announced its Road to Practitioner (R2P) program—a multi-week online quantum computing course beginning in May, taught by members of IBM’s quantum team. This is an excellent opportunity to learn about the current state of quantum computing and algorithms in a focused, instructor-led setting. Visit https://foci.rpi.edu/news-and-events for more details and the link to register.
item.author: Community

item.title: Earth Week Farmers Market April 23rd
item.description: Join Rensselaer Dining on Thursday, April 23rd for a Earth Week Farmers Market at the Student Union Patio from 11:00 am - 2:00 pm. Support local, eat fresh, and celebrate our planet together.
item.author: Community

item.title: Cognitive Science Colloquium with Dr. Fuat Balci on April 22nd
item.description: Please join the Cognitive Science Department on Wednesday, April 22nd, at 12:00 pm in Carnegie 113 for a presentation titled “The Stochastic Bridge: Diffusion in Timing and Time Awareness” by Dr. Fuat Balcı, Full Professor at the University of Manitoba and an internationally recognized expert in cognitive neuroscience.
item.author: Community

item.title: iEAR Presents! River of Grass Screening and Talk
item.description: Join us at The Sanctuary for Independent Media, 3361 6th Ave., Troy, NY, for a film screening of River of Grass, a documentary film which is described as “An ode to the Florida Everglades, told through the prescient writings of Marjory Stoneman Douglas, and those who today call the region home.” This film screening will be followed by a Q&A with filmmaker Sasha Wortzel who, ahead of the screening, will also offer a Be The Media! workshop called Community Collaboration and Reciprocity at 5pm.
item.author: Community

item.title: Corpus Festival | April 23-25, 2026
item.description: A weekend of creative experiments and gatherings that attune us to how bodily forms and bodies in formation construct our world. How do we take part in society by physically showing up? We all play our part in shaping power, control, freedom, and mutual responsibility.
item.author: Community

item.title: Dancing Land, Dancing Power
item.description: Friday / April 24, 2026 at 6:00 pm How does dance take up space in the world? Why pay attention to the histories of the places where people gather to create and watch choreography? What happens when those places are contested? Talk Arabella Stanger
item.author: Community

item.title: Pingkun Yan Inducted into the 2026 Class of the AIMBE College of Fellows
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 12:00 AM - 1:00 AM - Pingkun Yan, PhD. Professor and Department Head of Biomedical Engineering was inducted into the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering (AIMBE) 2026 College of Fellows on April 13, 2026 . This is among the highest professional distinctions in medical and biological engineering, making up the top two percent of engineers in this field.
item.author: Community

item.title: preámbulo
item.description: Friday / April 24, 2026 at 8:00 pm How does a site choreograph us? How might we re-choreograph it—or at least allow its architecture to hold our sense of time differently? Performance nibia pastrana santiago
item.author: Community

item.title: Mozart Violin Sonatas Concert
item.description: Join us this Sunday April 26, 2026, for a concert with Mozart violin sonatas, at 6:30 pm at the HASS Media Center (DCC 174). The concert is a collaboration between two instrumental studios at RPI, Jamecyn Morey’s violin and Chrysi Nanou’s piano studios and the Chamber Music Ensembles class.
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar-Friday April 24: Webex Training Session
item.description: Join us on Friday, April 24, from 11am-12pm for a hands-on Webex Training Session, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. This session focuses on the Webex application and its integration with Outlook and other productivity tools, so you can efficiently schedule, launch, and manage meetings directly from the tools you use every day. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: Additional Commencement Ticket Information
item.description: On Friday, April 24th, starting at 9:00 AM EDT, students who registered for Commencement can claim one additional ticket. Please note that there are only 500 tickets available due to the number of graduates and the venue’s capacity. To claim your additional ticket, log in to your Tassel account and return to the registration form to the “your tickets” page. If you have any questions, please reach out to commencement@rpi.edu.
item.author: Community

item.title: Queering RPI | Collecting Positive Queer Experiences on Campus
item.description: Queering RPI is a storytelling and community building project designed to highlight the experiences, memories, and perspectives of queer students, faculty, and staff at RPI. The goal is to show students that they are not alone, that their identities matter, and that there is space for them on campus. This project collects and shares anonymous anecdotes, reflections, and moments from queer members of the RPI community.
item.author: Community

item.title: QTPiE Emerging Scholar Grant – Call for Proposals
item.description: Queer and Trans People in Education (QTPiE) invites proposals from emerging scholars interested in queer and trans education research. This grant supports collaborative quantitative research that advances equity for queer and trans people in education. Proposals are due Friday, 5/1. Visit https://www.qtpiecollective.org for the full Call for Proposals and details. Questions? Email jcgarvey@uvm.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: Celebrate the Winning Teams with CEE
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 3:00 PM - 4:00 PM - The Civil Engineering Concrete Canoe team recently won 1st place overall at the ASCE Regional Competition. This is RPI’s first win in many years of competing. In more good news, two of our Environmental teams competed in the WERC Competition in New Mexico. Both teams won 1st Place overall in their respective categories. Come celebrate with us and congratulate the students in person while enjoying some refreshments. We will be in the JEC…
item.author: Community

item.title: EMPAC presents Corpus Festival — Come explore!
item.description: Date: Friday, April 24, 2026 (all day) - Across the EMPAC building, creative experiments in dance and theater explore power, control, freedom, mutual responsibility, and the potential to move differently. Join us to witness — and even participate in — experiences by rule-breaking artists. RSVP for the group activation of “Raft” by Yanira Castro | a canary torsi; the talk “Dancing Land, Dancing Power” from Arabella Stanger; and the performance “preámbulo” by nibia pastrana santiago. All festival…
item.author: Community

item.title: CS Expo
item.description: Date: Friday, April 24, 2026 4:00 AM - 7:00 AM - RCOS and Computer Science present the CS Expo, a gathering of undergraduate and graduate research poster presentations. Come and see what your friends have been working on in RCOS, in undergraduate research, and in other courses. Light refreshments will be available. We hope you’ll join us!
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Classification Best Practices
item.description: Information Classification is the process of categorizing data based on it’s sensitivity, value, and criticality to organizations, ensuring appropriate security controls are applied. Please click above link to review our Information Classification Best Practices and Policy.
item.author: Community

item.title: Training: Handshake for Faculty/Staff
item.description: Are you a faculty or staff member hiring for on-campus research or student assistant positions? Consider using Handshake as a recruiting tool! The CCPD will host a training session about using the system on Wednesday, May 20th from 12-12:45pm in their office, DCC 209. Advanced sign-up is required; email Nicole Doran (stongn@rpi.edu) before Wednesday, May 13th to register or learn more!
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: MBA for Professionals Review with RPI at Work
item.description: Date: Friday, April 24, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - Learn to lead from every aspect of an organization with a real-world MBA experience. Prepare to lead the most dynamic and innovative organizations. Engage, involve, motivate, and lead others to reach their fullest potential. Join RPI Dean Aric Krause, Ph. D., as he reviews this innovative project-based, online MBA for professionals program. Please register to receive login details to join this virtual event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Faculty & Staff Appreciation Lunch - April 22nd, 2026
item.description:
Join us on Wednesday April 22nd for a $6 (plus tax) lunch at Commons or Russell Sage Dining Halls as we get towards the end of another academic school year! We hope you can take a break, connect with colleagues, and enjoy this time together.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
